name: activity_projection_stub
docs: STUB pending the rust4pm `feat/oc-declare-act-projection` merge.
Kept as its own no-op binding so the app builds and runs on the
`feat/register-binding-downstream` branch before the engine branches are
consolidated. Replace with the real activity-projection implementation then.

name: app_ping
docs: Health-check binding — validates downstream registration in the real engine.

Ported from propel `get_removable_attributes_xes`.

sample_values.description: Up to 5 distinct sample values. Empty for OCEL.

name: get_trace_events
docs: Events and case-level attributes for a single trace. Ported from propel `get_trace_events`.
On an out-of-bounds `case_index` returns an empty `TraceDetail` (propel returned an `Err`;
the registry binding is infallible, so we yield empty rather than panic).

name: get_object_detail
docs: Detail (events, O2O, attributes) for a single object. Ported from propel `get_object_detail`.
On unknown `object_id` returns an empty `ObjectDetail` (propel returned an `Err`;
the registry binding is infallible).

name: get_dotted_chart
docs: Generate dotted chart data from an event log.
Traces are sorted by the timestamp of their first event. Each event
produces one dot with coordinates determined by the configured axes.

DottedChartColorAxis.description: Color-axis mode for the dotted chart.

oneOf.description: Color by a custom event attribute.
Color by a custom case (trace) attribute.

DottedChartXAxis.description: X-axis mode for the dotted chart.

oneOf.description: Elapsed time since the first event in the case (in milliseconds).
Relative position within the case duration (`0.0`..`1.0`).
Event index within the case (starting at `0`).

DottedChartYAxis.description: Y-axis mode for the dotted chart.

oneOf.description: Group by a custom event attribute.
Group by a custom case (trace) attribute.

args.description: Options for [`get_dotted_chart`].

timestamp_key.description: Event attribute key used to extract the timestamp.

args.title: DottedChartOptions

DottedChartPoints.description: A series of (x, y) coordinates for one color group in a dotted chart.

return_type.description: Result of [`get_dotted_chart`], containing the plotted points grouped by color.

y_values.description: Ordered list of y-axis labels (index corresponds to [`DottedChartPoints::y`] values).

return_type.title: DottedChartData

name: get_event_timestamps
docs: Aggregate event timestamps into a fixed number of bins, grouped by activity.
Events without a valid timestamp are skipped; events without a recognized
activity are counted under `"UNKNOWN"`.

args.description: Options for [`get_event_timestamps`].

activity_key.description: Event attribute key used to identify the activity name.

num_bins.description: Number of time bins to aggregate events into.

timestamp_key.description: Event attribute key used to extract the timestamp.

args.title: EventTimestampOptions

return_type.description: Aggregated event counts per timestamp bin, grouped by activity.

activities.description: All distinct activity names found in the log.

events_per_timestamp.description: Event counts per bin timestamp (millis) per activity name.

return_type.title: AggregatedEventTimestamps

name: get_object_attribute_changes
docs: Extract the attribute change history for a specific object.
Returns one trace per attribute, each containing the timestamped values
in the order they appear in the log. Returns an error if the object ID
is not found.

AttributeChange.description: A single attribute value change at a point in time.

value.description: Attribute value at this point in time.

return_type.description: Time-stamped attribute change history for a single OCEL object.

traces.description:
Attribute change traces keyed by attribute name.

Each entry contains the chronological list of value changes
for that attribute.

return_type.title: ObjectAttributeChanges

name: locel_oc_perf_sojourn_per_event
docs: Per-event sojourn time.
For each event with at least one directly-follows predecessor, the sojourn time is
`event_time - latest_predecessor_time` in integer microseconds. Returns one row
`(event_id, sojourn_us)` per qualifying event.
`top_k`: if `Some(k)`, return only the `k` rows with the largest `sojourn_us`, ties broken by
ascending event id, sorted descending. `None` returns every qualifying event.

name: locel_oc_perf_sync_per_event
docs: Per-event synchronization time and the delaying object.
For each event with at least one directly-follows predecessor, the synchronization time is
`max_predecessor_time - min_predecessor_time` in integer microseconds (the span between its
earliest and latest directly-preceding event). The delaying object is the object linking the
latest predecessor (ties broken by ascending object id).
Returns one row `(event_id, sync_us, delaying_object_id)` per qualifying event.
`top_k`: if `Some(k)`, return only the `k` rows with the largest `sync_us`, ties broken by
ascending event id, sorted descending. `None` returns every qualifying event.

name: locel_conversion_rate
docs: Conversion rate from `source_type` to `target_type` via O2O, restricted to targets touched by `activity`.
Returns the fraction of `source_type` objects that have at least one outgoing O2O edge to a
`target_type` object related (via E2O) to some event of the given event type. Returns `0.0`
if no `source_type` objects exist.

name: locel_event_object_type_counts
docs: Count E2O relationships per `(event_type, object_type)` pair.
Each entry is `(event_type, object_type, count)`. A single `(event, object)` pair connected
by multiple qualifiers contributes once per qualifier. Pairs with zero relations are omitted.
Result row order is unspecified.

args.description:
An Object Index

Points to an object in the context of a given OCEL
args.title: ObjectIndex

name: locel_add_e2o
docs: Add an E2O (event-to-object) relationship with the given qualifier.
Multiple qualifiers between the same `(event, object)` pair are allowed; re-adding the exact
same `(event, object, qualifier)` triple is a no-op. Returns `true` on success, `false` if
either index is out of bounds (with a stderr warning).

args.description:
An Event Index

Points to an event in the context of a given OCEL
args.title: EventIndex

args.description:
An Object Index

Points to an object in the context of a given OCEL
args.title: ObjectIndex

name: locel_add_event
docs: Add an event and return its [`EventIndex`].
The event type must have been declared via [`locel_add_event_type`] first;
otherwise this returns `None`.
`id`: If `None`, a UUID is assigned. Returns `None` if the id is already taken.
`attributes`: Positional values in the declared attribute order. Padded with `Null` or truncated on length mismatch (with a warning).
`relationships`: E2O relationships as `(qualifier, object_index)` pairs (can also be added later via [`locel_add_e2o`]).

ObjectIndex.description:
An Object Index

Points to an object in the context of a given OCEL

EventIndex.description:
An Event Index

Points to an event in the context of a given OCEL

name: locel_add_event_type
docs: Add an event type with the given ordered attribute declarations.
No-op if the event type already exists.

name: locel_add_o2o
docs: Add a directed O2O (object-to-object) relationship from `from_obj` to `to_obj` with the given qualifier.
Multiple qualifiers between the same `(from_obj, to_obj)` pair are allowed; re-adding the exact
same `(from_obj, to_obj, qualifier)` triple is a no-op. Returns `true` on success, `false` if
either index is out of bounds (with a stderr warning).

args.description:
An Object Index

Points to an object in the context of a given OCEL
args.title: ObjectIndex

args.description:
An Object Index

Points to an object in the context of a given OCEL
args.title: ObjectIndex

name: locel_add_object
docs: Add an object and return its [`ObjectIndex`].
The object type must have been declared via [`locel_add_object_type`] first;
otherwise this returns `None`.
`id`: If `None`, a UUID is assigned. Returns `None` if the id is already taken.

`attributes`: Positional list of time-indexed attribute histories (one `(timestamp, value)` list per declared attribute, in order). Use `1970-01-01T00:00:00Z` for constant/initial values. Padded with empty lists or truncated on length mismatch (with a warning).
`relationships`: Outgoing O2O relationships as `(qualifier, object_index)` pairs (can also be added later via [`locel_add_o2o`]).

ObjectIndex.description:
An Object Index

Points to an object in the context of a given OCEL

An Object Index

Points to an object in the context of a given OCEL

name: locel_add_object_type
docs: Add an object type with the given ordered attribute declarations.

docs: Reconstruct a full [`OCEL`] from a [`SlimLinkedOCEL`]. Can be expensive for large logs.

name: locel_new
docs: Create a new empty [`SlimLinkedOCEL`].
A [`SlimLinkedOCEL`] is an object-centric event log where events and objects are referenced
by integer indices ([`EventIndex`] / [`ObjectIndex`]) returned from the `add_*` calls,
and each indexed event/object is an instance of an event/object type (activity / object class)
declared beforehand with an ordered list of attributes.

name: align_empty_trace
docs: Align the empty trace to the given model
with the specified options
Permits at most 255 ([`petri_net::TokenCount::MAX`]) tokens in each place.

Arc.description:
Arc in a Petri net

Connecting a transition and a place (or the other way around)

weight.description: Weight (i.e., how many tokens this arc moves)

ArcType.description: Arc type in a Petri net

label.description: Transition label (None if this transition is _invisible_)

args.description:
A Petri net of [`Place`]s and [`Transition`]s

Bipartite graph of [`Place`]s and [`Transition`]s with [`Arc`]s connecting them, as well as initial and final [`Marking`]s

final_markings.description: Final markings (any of them are accepted as a final marking)

args.title: PetriNet

CostFunction.description:
Cost function for alignment moves.

Defines the cost of model moves, log moves, synchronous moves, and silent moves.

log_move_cost.description: Default cost for a log move (log event not matched by model)

model_move_cost.description: Default cost for a model move (visible transition fires without matching log event)

silent_move_cost.description: Default cost for a silent/tau move

sync_move_cost.description: Default cost for a synchronous move

args.description: Options for computing alignment

max_states.description:
Maximum number of states to visit before aborting (per trace).
`None` means no limit.

args.title: AlignmentOptions

trace_event_index.description: Index of the event in the trace
Index of the event in the trace

states_visited.description: Number of states visited during search

name: oc_declare_conformance
docs: Returns the confidence conformance of an OC-DECLARE arc on the given OCEL
Returns a value from 0.0 (all source events violate this constraint) to 1.0 (all source events satisfy this constraint)

OCDeclareArcLabel.description: Object Involvement Label of an OC-DECLARE arc

all.description: All (there must be the specified number of relevant target events involving all of the objects of this type involved in the source event)

any.description: Any (there must be the specified number of relevant target events involving at least one of the objects of this type involved in the source event)

each.description: Each (for each object of that type separately, there must be the specified number of relevant target events)

OCDeclareArcType.description:
OC-DECLARE Arc Direction/Type

Models temporal relationships

oneOf.description: Eventually-Follows: Target must occur after source event
Eventually-Precedes: Target must occur before source event
Directly-Follows: Target must occur directly after source event (considering events that involve all required objects)
Directly-Precedes: Target must occur directly before source event (considering events that involve all required objects)

ObjectTypeAssociation.description: Object Type Association: Direct or O2O Object Types

oneOf.description: Simple: Direct Object Types involved with an Activity
Indirect: Object Association through an O2O relationship

reversed.description:
Specifies the direction of the O2O relationship.

If reversed is `False`, `(first,second)` is considered

args.description: OC-DECLARE Constraint arc/edge between two nodes (i.e., activities)

counts.description: First tuple element: min count (optional), Second: max count (optional)

label.description: Arc label specifying object involvement criteria

args.title: OCDeclareArc

name: get_num_cases
docs: Get the total number of cases in the projection

name: get_num_variants
docs: Get the number of distinct trace variants in the projection

name: get_projection_activities
docs: Get the list of all activity names in the projection

name: get_top_n_variants
docs: Get the `n` most frequent process variants, sorted by frequency (descending)
Each variant includes the activity sequence as names, its count, and percentage of total cases.
To get all variants, pass `n` = [`get_num_variants`] or use the [`get_variants`] function.
To get only the most frequent variant, pass `n` = 1.
Assumes `projection.traces` is sorted by descending frequency, as maintained by the provided
constructors.

ProcessVariant.description: A process variant (activity sequence with its frequency)

activities.description: The activity sequence of the variant as activity names

count.description: Number of cases corresponding to this variant

percentage.description: Percentage of total cases corresponding to this variant

name: get_variants
docs: Get all process variants, sorted by frequency (descending)
Each variant includes the activity sequence as names, its count, and percentage of total cases.
Assumes `projection.traces` is sorted by descending frequency, as maintained by the provided
constructors.

ProcessVariant.description: A process variant (activity sequence with its frequency)

activities.description: The activity sequence of the variant as activity names

count.description: Number of cases corresponding to this variant

percentage.description: Percentage of total cases corresponding to this variant

name: log_to_activity_projection
docs: Convert an [`EventLog`] into an [`EventLogActivityProjection`]
All traces with the same activity sequence are aggregated into one trace with a frequency count.
This effectively constructs all trace variants, with their count.

name: flatten_ocel_on
docs: Flatten an OCEL on a specific object type, resulting in a case-centric Event Log
For each object of the specified type, a trace is created containing all events related to that object,
ordered by their timestamp.

name: add_init_exit_events_to_ocel
docs: Add artificial init/exit events to an OCEL
- `<init>` events are added for each object, exactly or slightly before the time they first occur in an event
- `<exit>` events are added for each object, exactly or slightly after the time they last occur in an event
For an object of type `order` the activities are then called `<init> order` or `<exit> order`, respectively.
__Note: This processing is no longer necessary for OC-DECLARE discovery and conformance checking__
This function remains, as it might be useful for other applications.

type.description: Event Type (referring back to the `name` of an [`OCELType`])
Object Type (referring back to thte `name` of an [`OCELType`])

OCELObjectAttribute.description:
OCEL Object Attribute

Describing a named value _at a certain point in time_

OCELRelationship.description: OCEL Relationship (qualified; referring back to an [`OCELObject`])

args.description:
Object-centric Event Log

Consists of multiple [`OCELEvent`]s and [`OCELObject`]s with corresponding event and object [`OCELType`]s
args.title: OCEL

name: discover_dfg_from_ocel
docs: Construct an [`OCDirectlyFollowsGraph`] from an OCEL

DirectlyFollowsGraph.description:
A directly-follows graph of [`Activity`]s.
Graph containing a set of activities, a set of directly-follows relations, a set of start
activities, and a set of end activities.
Both, the number of occurrences of activities and of directly follows relations are annotated
with their frequency.

return_type.description:
An object-centric directly-follows graph containing a [`DirectlyFollowsGraph`] for each object
type involved.
return_type.title: OCDirectlyFollowsGraph

name: discover_alpha+++
docs: Discover a [`PetriNet`] using the Alpha+++ Process Discovery algorithm

args.description: Algorithm parameters for Alpha+++

absolute_df_clean_thresh.description: Absolute threshold for weighted DFG cleaning

log_repair_loop_df_thresh_rel.description: Log repair threshold for loops (wrt. to weighted DFG)

log_repair_skip_df_thresh_rel.description: Log repair threshold for skips (wrt. to weighted DFG)

relative_df_clean_thresh.description: Relative threshold for weighted DFG cleaning

args.title: AlphaPPPConfig

Arc.description:
Arc in a Petri net

Connecting a transition and a place (or the other way around)

weight.description: Weight (i.e., how many tokens this arc moves)

ArcType.description: Arc type in a Petri net

label.description: Transition label (None if this transition is _invisible_)

return_type.description:
A Petri net of [`Place`]s and [`Transition`]s

Bipartite graph of [`Place`]s and [`Transition`]s with [`Arc`]s connecting them, as well as initial and final [`Marking`]s

final_markings.description: Final markings (any of them are accepted as a final marking)

return_type.title: PetriNet

name: discover_dfg
docs: Discover [`DirectlyFollowsGraph`] with default classifier

return_type.description:
A directly-follows graph of [`Activity`]s.
Graph containing a set of activities, a set of directly-follows relations, a set of start
activities, and a set of end activities.
Both, the number of occurrences of activities and of directly follows relations are annotated
with their frequency.
return_type.title: DirectlyFollowsGraph

name: get_dfg_of_object_type
docs: Get the directly-follows graph (DFG) for objects of the given object type.
Each entry is `((from_activity, to_activity), count)`, counting adjacent pairs in each
object's timestamp-ordered activity trace. Sorted by count descending, ties broken by
`(from_activity, to_activity)`.

name: discover_oc_declare
docs: Discover behavioral OC-DECLARE constraints from the given OCEL

O2OMode.description:
O2O Mode for OC-DECLARE Discovery

Determines to what extent object-to-object (O2O) relationships are considered

oneOf.description: O2O relationships are not considered at all
Direct O2O relationships are considered (i.e., only one direction)
Reverse direction of O2O relationships are considered
O2O relationships and their inverse directions are considered

OCDeclareArcType.description:
OC-DECLARE Arc Direction/Type

Models temporal relationships

oneOf.description: Eventually-Follows: Target must occur after source event
Eventually-Precedes: Target must occur before source event
Directly-Follows: Target must occur directly after source event (considering events that involve all required objects)
Directly-Precedes: Target must occur directly before source event (considering events that involve all required objects)
Do not reduce constraints at all

Apply lossless reduction

i.e., only removes constraints strictly implied by combining others

Apply lossy reduction

May also remove constraints which are not implied by others

args.description: Options for the automatic discovery of OC-DECLARE constraints

acts_to_use.description: Activities to use for the discovery. If this is `None`, all activities of the OCEL are used

considered_arrow_types.description:
The arrow types to consider when deriving the final constraints

Should be non-empty!

counts_for_filter.description: What min/max counts to use for the candidate filtering step (when the arrow type is determined)

counts_for_generation.description: What min/max counts to use for the candidate generation steps

noise_threshold.description: Noise threshold (i.e., what fraction of events are allowed to violate a discovered constraint)

o2o_mode.description: Determines if/how object-to-object relationships are considered

reduction.description: If/how the discovered constraints should be reduced

refinement.description: Determines if the object involvement of discovered constraints should be made more precise/strict after initial discovery and reduction

args.title: OCDeclareDiscoveryOptions

OCDeclareArc.description: OC-DECLARE Constraint arc/edge between two nodes (i.e., activities)

counts.description: First tuple element: min count (optional), Second: max count (optional)

label.description: Arc label specifying object involvement criteria

OCDeclareArcLabel.description: Object Involvement Label of an OC-DECLARE arc

all.description: All (there must be the specified number of relevant target events involving all of the objects of this type involved in the source event)

any.description: Any (there must be the specified number of relevant target events involving at least one of the objects of this type involved in the source event)

each.description: Each (for each object of that type separately, there must be the specified number of relevant target events)

OCDeclareArcType.description:
OC-DECLARE Arc Direction/Type

Models temporal relationships

oneOf.description: Eventually-Follows: Target must occur after source event
Eventually-Precedes: Target must occur before source event
Directly-Follows: Target must occur directly after source event (considering events that involve all required objects)
Directly-Precedes: Target must occur directly before source event (considering events that involve all required objects)

ObjectTypeAssociation.description: Object Type Association: Direct or O2O Object Types

oneOf.description: Simple: Direct Object Types involved with an Activity
Indirect: Object Association through an O2O relationship

reversed.description:
Specifies the direction of the O2O relationship.

If reversed is `False`, `(first,second)` is considered

name: get_variants_of_object_type
docs: Get all activity-trace variants for objects of the given object type, with their occurrence counts
Each entry is a tuple `(activity_trace, count)`, where `activity_trace` is the sequence of event types
connected to an object (ordered by event timestamp), and `count` is the number of objects of the
requested type that share that exact trace. Sorted by count descending, ties broken by the
activity trace.
